title: Physics-Constrained Booster
summary: Tyre wear, degradation pressure, and fresh-tyre advantage have partly monotone relationships with pit likelihood.
rationale: Tyre wear, degradation pressure, and fresh-tyre advantage have partly monotone relationships with pit likelihood. XGBoost/CatBoost both support monotone constraints, and XGBoost also supports interaction constraints to prevent spurious high-cardinality interactions.
implementation_hint: Train a specialist booster with monotone constraints on wear-pressure features such as TyreLife, positive LapTime_Delta, degradation-per-lap, finish-excess, and decreasing fresh-tyre finish-margin features. Add interaction constraints so wear variables mainly interact with Compound, RaceProgress, and regime flags, then rank-average with the unconstrained baseline.
expected_effect: Likely reduces overfit to noisy race/driver patterns and narrows CV-to-LB drift while keeping strong discrimination.
risk: Wrong constraint directions or overly tight interaction sets can underfit and miss real nonlinearities.
